title: thelypteris subtruncata
intro: Thelypteris subtruncata, also known as the truncated maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Tinctures, infusions, and salves are commonly prepared from the plant's rhizomes and leaves to harness its medicinal benefits.
